channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for April 3, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Drone Hits Kuwaiti Oil Refinery in New Attack on Gulf Energy Sites
item.description: The strike set several refinery units ablaze, its operator said. President Trump threatened to destroy Iran’s infrastructure but there was no sign of a deal to end the war.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Students Are Disciplined Over Antisemitic Posts Targeting Hockey Rivals
item.description: A Connecticut high school said that it was aware of the Instagram posts and that antisemitism is “repugnant and antithetical to our values as a school.”
item.dc:creator: Katherine Rosman

media:content.media:description: The Fairfield College Preparatory School hockey team, from Fairfield, Conn., huddled before a championship game against New Canaan High School last month.

item.title: Picture Prompt | A Sneeze
item.description: What do you think this image is communicating?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Una historia secreta de la psicosis
item.description: Cohen Miles-Rath escuchó voces que le decían que matara a su padre y luego pasó años reconstruyendo el camino de sus delirios.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Barry

media:content.media:credit: Celia Talbot Tobin for The New York Times

item.title: How New Yorkers Spend, Splurge and Scrimp to Live in the City
item.description: Eliza Shapiro, who reports on New York City’s affordability crisis, asked hundreds of residents to get candid about their finances.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr

media:content.media:credit: Anna Watts for The New York Times

item.title: Myanmar Junta Chief Ascends to President, Five Years After Coup
item.description: U Min Aung Hlaing’s elevation to the civilian post is the conclusion of elections in the country, which were stage managed by the military.
item.dc:creator: Richard C. Paddock

media:content.media:description: Myanmar’s military ruler, Min Aung Hlaing, after casting hist vote in Naypyidaw, the capital, in December. He was elected president on Friday by the country’s rubber-stamp Parliament.

item.title: Qué significa inculcate
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 12 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: inculcate
item.description: This word has appeared in 11 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Late Night Says Goodbye to Pam Bondi
item.description: President Trump is “on a bit of a firing spree,” Jimmy Fallon said on “The Tonight Show” on Thursday, adding, “Ironically, the only staffer who has immunity is RFK Jr.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: How Giorgia Meloni Fell to Earth
item.description: For more than three years, Giorgia Meloni’s leadership of Italy has seemed unshakable. Now she suddenly seems vulnerable.
item.dc:creator: Anna Momigliano
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni of Italy has positioned herself as someone who can curry favor with President Trump.

item.title: Cosmopolitan Shanghai Is an Uneasy Fit in China’s Narrative of Western Sins
item.description: Shanghai’s many layers of architecture, culture and politics have made it a difficult fit for the Communist Party’s preferred narrative of Chinese victimhood and Western sins.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins and Qilai Shen

media:content.media:description: The Shanghai History Museum, housed in the former Shanghai Race Club.

item.title: In Syria, Kidnappings of Women and Girls Fuel a Minority Group’s Fears
item.description: A Times investigation found that abductions of women and girls from Syria’s Alawite minority were more common, and more brutal, than the government has acknowledged.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard and Laura Boushnak

media:content.media:description: A 16-year-old girl who said she was kidnapped and raped, and later found out she was pregnant.

item.title: They Re-Planned Their Wedding in 10 Days
item.description: After cartel violence erupted in Mexico, just weeks before their wedding in Punta Mita, Lauren Futterman and Tyler Olson quickly decided to move the ceremony to California.
item.dc:creator: Donna Kim

item.title: May I End My Life With You?
item.description: My friend made the weightiest decision possible while staying at my home. I struggled with what it meant.
item.dc:creator: Julie Rehmeyer

item.title: First the Hora, Then the Hora Loca
item.description: Dani Roldan and Michael Wexler brought their families together, blending Jewish and Colombian traditions at their Miami wedding.
item.dc:creator: Donna Kim

item.title: He Likes the Mets, She the Yankees. That’s Where Their Differences End.
item.description: After connecting online, Lisa Tarantino and Billy Peeker bonded over their Long Island upbringings and their love of movies, breweries and baseball.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora

item.title: After They Finally Met, Their ‘Rom-Com Story’ Began
item.description: Jacqueline Lobel and Michka Bengio started dating during the pandemic, though the two had briefly connected a few years before.
item.dc:creator: Alix Strauss

item.title: Quote of the Day: MACRON IS VOICE FOR FRUSTRATION OVER WAR IN IRAN
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Friday, April 3, 2026.

item.title: Cuba Says It Is Pardoning More Than 2,000 Prisoners
item.description: The Cuban government said the releases were a humanitarian gesture during Holy Week. It was not clear if they were related to ongoing negotiations between Cuba and the United States.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado and David C. Adams

media:content.media:description: Havana in January. The Cuban Embassy in Washington described the latest pardons as a “humanitarian and sovereign gesture.”

item.title: Pam Bondi Wanted a Graceful Exit. But Trump Wanted Her Gone.
item.description: Pam Bondi had a feeling her days as attorney general were numbered. But she didn’t expect President Trump to drop the curtain quite so soon.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Thrush and Tyler Pager

media:content.media:description: Attorney General Pam Bondi at a cabinet meeting in January. She met with President Trump on Wednesday to make her case to stay on.

item.title: Megan Thee Stallion Makes Her Return to “Moulin Rouge”
item.description: The rapper was back onstage in “Moulin Rouge” two days after being taken to a hospital after becoming ill during a performance.
item.dc:creator: Elena Bergeron

media:content.media:description: Megan Thee Stallion was back onstage at Broadway’s Al Hirschfeld Theater on Thursday night.

item.title: Trump’s Strait of Hormuz Problem
item.description: Our White House reporter Zolan Kanno-Youngs parses some of President Trump’s conflicting messages about the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Christina Thornell, Coleman Lowndes and Whitney Shefte
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: ‘The Pitt’ Season 2 Episode 13 Recap: Should He Stay or Should He Go?
item.description: Dr. Robby finally says what his colleagues had been thinking.
item.dc:creator: Sean T. Collins

media:content.media:description: Noah Wyle in “The Pitt.”

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for April 3, 2026
item.description: Carolyn Davies Lynch and Brian Callahan open our solving weekend.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

item.title: Corrections: April 3, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Friday, April 3, 2026.

item.title: Did Jesus Rise From the Dead?
item.description: A case of mistaken identity, or a profound experience of grief? The religious scholar Bart Ehrman argues that the earliest accounts of Jesus’ Resurrection might be rooted in the way we process loss.
item.dc:creator: Interesting Times

item.title: Gucci Mane Held at Gunpoint, Prompting 2 Rappers’ Arrests
item.description: The hip-hop titan was forced to sign papers releasing a rapper known as Pooh Shiesty from his record contract during an armed confrontation in January at a Dallas music studio, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor

media:content.media:description: Gucci Mane performing in Atlanta last year.

item.title: How Jesus Altered the World’s Conscience
item.description: It might sound obvious now, but helping your neighbor wasn’t always seen as a moral imperative. The New Testament scholar Bart Ehrman explains how Jesus’ teachings shape the way we all view our role in society today.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Thursday
item.description: President Trump’s prime-time address received a defiant response from a top leader in Tehran.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Amit Elkayam for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Workers inspecting the damage from a strike by Iran on Petah Tikva, Israel, on Thursday.

item.title: Hegseth Allows Troops to Carry Personal Firearms at Military Bases
item.description: Many shootings at military bases have involved troops bringing guns they purchased onto base grounds and opening fire on fellow service members.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Paul Ratje for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth during a visit to the U.S.-Mexico border last year.

item.title: Trump Says Opening the Strait of Hormuz ‘Should Be Easy.’ Will It?
item.description: President Trump is asking U.S. allies to “go to” the Strait of Hormuz “and just take it.” Eric Schmitt, our national security correspondent, explains how difficult it would be to clear the strait by force.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Gilad Thaler, Christina Shaman, Laura Salaberry, Rafaela Balster, Whitney Shefte, Adina Renner and Samuel Granados

item.title: Hegseth Fires Army Chief Amid Battle With Its Leaders
item.description: Senior Army officers reacted with anger and frustration to news of Gen. Randy George’s dismissal, characterizing it as the latest blow to the service.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe, Helene Cooper and Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Nick Hagen for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gen. Randy George is expected to be replaced by Gen. Christopher LaNeve, who previously served as Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s senior military assistant in the Pentagon.

item.title: President Trump Fires Pam Bondi as Attorney General
item.description: On Thursday, President Trump announced on social media that Attorney General Pam Bondi would be leaving her role for a new position in the private sector.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Leventhal and Shawn Paik

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Pam Bondi’s dismissal makes her the second cabinet member to lose her job in recent weeks.

item.title: ICE Arrests the Head of Wisconsin’s Largest Islamic Group
item.description: Officials detained Salah Sarsour, the president of the Islamic Society of Milwaukee, saying he had been convicted of crimes by Israel and had lied on a green card application in the 1990s.
item.dc:creator: Jacey Fortin

media:content.media:credit: via the Islamic Society of Milwaukee
media:content.media:description: Salah Sarsour, the president of the Islamic Society of Milwaukee, came to the United States nearly 33 years ago.

item.title: What to Know About the ‘Massive’ Military Bunker Beneath Trump’s Ballroom
item.description: President Trump has been talking about the emergency facility beneath what was once the East Wing, details of which are usually kept secret, as he tries to justify his renovation.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater

media:content.media:description: President Trump shared some of his plans for the bunker, referring to the secure underground facility as a “massive complex.”

item.title: Johnson Wavers on Ending the Shutdown, Reflecting His Weak Hold on Power
item.description: The House speaker first panned, then endorsed, then punted on, then pitched and now is delaying a bill to reopen the Homeland Security Department, showing his vulnerability in the face of party rifts.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Speaker Mike Johnson has defined his role as a tool to further President Trump’s power and cheerlead his agenda, and in turn relied on the president to help him corral Republicans on tough votes.

item.title: Macron Voices Europe’s Frustration With Trump’s Handling of Iran War
item.description: President Emmanuel Macron of France expressed disapproval about President Trump’s handling of the war against Iran on Thursday, chastising him for speaking cavalierly in a speech.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler, Erika Solomon and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: President Emmanuel Macron of France in Seoul on Thursday. Addressing President Trump’s verbal attacks against NATO, he said, “If you create doubt every day about your commitment, you hollow it out.”

item.title: The Awe of a Moon Launch in an Age of Trump, Turmoil and Tribal Divisions
item.description: The launch of Artemis II captured the tenor of the times in a country that can still do big things but seems forever mired in big problems.
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker

media:content.media:description: NASA’s Artemis II rocket on the launchpad at Kennedy Space Center in Cape Canaveral, Fla., on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump Fires His Attorney General
item.description: Also, France’s president voices European frustration with the U.S. leader. Here’s the latest at the end of Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times

item.title: Rival Nations Seize On Choke Points to Counter Trump
item.description: From Iran to China, President Trump’s global aggression has encouraged other countries to search for new ways to pressure the U.S. economy.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump has claimed that his international aggression has only upsides.

item.title: Artemis II Astronauts Fly Away From Earth Toward the Moon
item.description: After a day of testing the Orion capsule high above the planet, the crew of the NASA mission became the first people to head into deep space since 1972.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Strikes Hit Infrastructure Sites in Iran After Trump’s Threat
item.description: President Trump celebrated an attack on a major highway bridge outside of Tehran after vowing to take Iran “back to the Stone Ages.” And a leading public health institution in the country was destroyed.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Amit Elkayam for The New York Times
